query:
Assume there is a 200 MW transmission-connected data center in Houston, TX that is exposed to day-ahead ERCOT Houston Hub market prices, what would be its total annual electricity cost considering energy and transmission-related charges only in 2025? Find the applicable transmission rate in ERCOT for 2025 and 4CP rules apply. The representative hourly profiles for the data center for each season are as follows and this applies to any year:
Winter: [0.62, 0.60, 0.58, 0.57, 0.58, 0.62, 0.68, 0.74, 0.81, 0.88, 0.94, 0.98, 1.00, 1.00, 0.99, 0.97, 0.95, 0.93, 0.90, 0.86, 0.81, 0.75, 0.70, 0.66],
Spring: [0.58, 0.56, 0.54, 0.53, 0.54, 0.58, 0.64, 0.71, 0.79, 0.87, 0.94, 0.99, 1.00, 1.00, 1.00, 0.99, 0.97, 0.95, 0.92, 0.88, 0.83, 0.77, 0.71, 0.64],
Summer: [0.55, 0.53, 0.51, 0.50, 0.51, 0.55, 0.61, 0.69, 0.78, 0.88, 0.96, 1.00, 1.00, 1.00, 1.00, 1.00, 0.99, 0.97, 0.95, 0.91, 0.86, 0.80, 0.73, 0.61],
Fall: [0.58, 0.56, 0.54, 0.53, 0.54, 0.58, 0.64, 0.71, 0.79, 0.87, 0.94, 0.99, 1.00, 1.00, 1.00, 0.99, 0.97, 0.95, 0.92, 0.88, 0.83, 0.77, 0.71, 0.64]
final_answer:
## Result

For a 200 MW transmission-connected Houston data center exposed to ERCOT Houston Hub day-ahead prices, I estimate **2025 annual electricity cost for energy + transmission-related charges only** at:

> **$61.55 million in 2025**  
> **= $48.60 million energy + $12.95 million transmission**

This equals an all-in energy-plus-transmission cost of about **$44.45/MWh** over the modeled 2025 load.

---

## Key assumptions

1. **Load shape:** I scaled the user-provided seasonal hourly profile by **200 MW**. Source: user-provided profiles.
2. **Season mapping:** Winter = Dec–Feb, Spring = Mar–May, Summer = Jun–Aug, Fall = Sep–Nov. Assumption.
3. **Energy price:** ERCOT **Houston Hub day-ahead hourly LMPs** for 2025. Source: `ercot_db.ercot_lmp`, filtered to `zone_name='houston'`, `zone_type='hub'`, `is_day_ahead=true`, 2025 calendar year.
4. **4CP billing rule:** 2025 transmission billing demand is based on the **previous year’s average 4CP** demand, i.e., the load coincident with ERCOT system peaks in June, July, August, and September of 2024. Source: 16 Tex. Admin. Code §25.192(d), which states the current year’s average 4CP demand is used to bill transmission service for the next year and defines the ERCOT average 4CP as the average of coincident peaks in June–September. URL: https://www.law.cornell.edu/regulations/texas/16-Tex-Admin-Code-SS-25-192
5. **2025 ERCOT transmission rate:** I used **$66.76/kW-year** as the ERCOT-wide 2025 transmission rate. Source: NRG “Transmission in ERCOT: Are Residential Customers Paying Too Much?” Feb. 25, 2025, stating “ERCOT’s transmission rate is currently $66.76 per kilowatt-year,” citing Commission Staff’s petition to set wholesale transmission service charges. URL: https://www.nrg.com/assets/documents/energy-policy/ercot-transmission-costs-and-rate-design-remarks-on-feb-25-2025.pdf. The relevant PUCT docket is **Docket No. 57491**, “Commission Staff’s Petition to Set 2025 Wholesale Transmission Service Charges for ERCOT.” Source: Texas PUC Interchange search result for Control No. 57491: https://interchange.puc.texas.gov/search/filings/?ControlNumber=57491
6. Excludes all other charges: distribution, losses, ancillary services, ERCOT admin/uplift, taxes, REP margin, power factor/reactive charges, backup service, and interconnection-specific charges. Assumption based on the user’s “energy and transmission-related charges only” instruction.

---

## Energy cost calculation

Using the modeled hourly load and 2025 ERCOT Houston Hub day-ahead LMPs:

| Metric | Value | Source |
|---|---:|---|
| Annual modeled load | **1,384,578 MWh** | User load profile scaled to 200 MW; hourly calculation |
| 2025 Houston Hub DA load-weighted price | **$35.10/MWh** | `ercot_db.ercot_lmp` |
| Energy cost | **$48,596,802** | `ercot_db.ercot_lmp` × modeled load |

Seasonal energy-cost detail:

| Season | MWh | Load-weighted Houston Hub DA price | Energy cost |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| Winter | 344,160 | $30.07/MWh | $10,347,355 |
| Spring | 348,204 | $37.65/MWh | $13,109,809 |
| Summer | 347,576 | $38.49/MWh | $13,379,833 |
| Fall | 344,638 | $34.12/MWh | $11,759,805 |
| **Total** | **1,384,578** | **$35.10/MWh** | **$48,596,802** |

Source for hourly prices: `ercot_db.ercot_lmp`, 2025, Houston Hub, day-ahead.

---

## Transmission 4CP calculation

Under ERCOT 4CP rules, transmission billing is based on the customer’s average coincident demand during ERCOT’s June, July, August, and September system peaks. Source: 16 Tex. Admin. Code §25.192(d): https://www.law.cornell.edu/regulations/texas/16-Tex-Admin-Code-SS-25-192

For 2025 transmission billing, I used the 2024 ERCOT system 4CP hours from `ercot_db.ercot_load`:

| Month | ERCOT system peak hour used | ERCOT load | Data center profile hour | Data center demand |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| June 2024 | 2024-06-30 17:00 | 79,697.9 MW | Summer hour 17 = 0.97 | 194 MW |
| July 2024 | 2024-07-01 17:00 | 81,010.1 MW | Summer hour 17 = 0.97 | 194 MW |
| August 2024 | 2024-08-20 17:00 | 85,198.9 MW | Summer hour 17 = 0.97 | 194 MW |
| September 2024 | 2024-09-19 16:00 | 77,779.5 MW | Fall hour 16 = 0.97 | 194 MW |

Average 4CP demand:

\[
\frac{194 + 194 + 194 + 194}{4} = 194 \text{ MW}
\]

Transmission charge:

\[
194{,}000 \text{ kW} \times 66.76 \frac{\$}{\text{kW-year}}
= \$12{,}951{,}440
\]

Sources: `ercot_db.ercot_load` for ERCOT system peak hours; user-provided load profiles; $66.76/kW-year from NRG Feb. 25, 2025 remarks citing PUCT 2025 wholesale transmission charge petition.

---

## Total 2025 cost

| Component | Cost |
|---|---:|
| Energy at ERCOT Houston Hub day-ahead prices | **$48,596,802** |
| ERCOT 4CP transmission charge | **$12,951,440** |
| **Total annual electricity cost, energy + transmission only** | **$61,548,242** |

\[
\text{Effective cost} =
\frac{61{,}548{,}242}{1{,}384{,}578 \text{ MWh}}
= \mathbf{44.45 \$/MWh}
\]

**Final answer: approximately $61.55 million for 2025, or $44.45/MWh, considering energy and ERCOT 4CP transmission-related charges only.**

WITH p AS (
  SELECT * FROM (VALUES
    (0, 0.62,0.58,0.55,0.58),(1,0.60,0.56,0.53,0.56),(2,0.58,0.54,0.51,0.54),(3,0.57,0.53,0.50,0.53),
    (4,0.58,0.54,0.51,0.54),(5,0.62,0.58,0.55,0.58),(6,0.68,0.64,0.61,0.64),(7,0.74,0.71,0.69,0.71),
    (8,0.81,0.79,0.78,0.79),(9,0.88,0.87,0.88,0.87),(10,0.94,0.94,0.96,0.94),(11,0.98,0.99,1.00,0.99),
    (12,1.00,1.00,1.00,1.00),(13,1.00,1.00,1.00,1.00),(14,0.99,1.00,1.00,1.00),(15,0.97,0.99,1.00,0.99),
    (16,0.95,0.97,0.99,0.97),(17,0.93,0.95,0.97,0.95),(18,0.90,0.92,0.95,0.92),(19,0.86,0.88,0.91,0.88),
    (20,0.81,0.83,0.86,0.83),(21,0.75,0.77,0.80,0.77),(22,0.70,0.71,0.73,0.71),(23,0.66,0.64,0.61,0.64)
  ) AS v(hr,winter,spring,summer,fall)
), hourly AS (
  SELECT l.timestamp, l.lmp,
         200.0 * CASE
           WHEN EXTRACT(MONTH FROM l.timestamp) IN (12,1,2) THEN p.winter
           WHEN EXTRACT(MONTH FROM l.timestamp) IN (3,4,5) THEN p.spring
           WHEN EXTRACT(MONTH FROM l.timestamp) IN (6,7,8) THEN p.summer
           ELSE p.fall END AS load_mw,
         CASE
           WHEN EXTRACT(MONTH FROM l.timestamp) IN (12,1,2) THEN 'Winter'
           WHEN EXTRACT(MONTH FROM l.timestamp) IN (3,4,5) THEN 'Spring'
           WHEN EXTRACT(MONTH FROM l.timestamp) IN (6,7,8) THEN 'Summer'
           ELSE 'Fall' END AS season
  FROM ercot_lmp l
  JOIN p ON p.hr = EXTRACT(HOUR FROM l.timestamp)::int
  WHERE l.is_day_ahead = true
    AND l.zone_name = 'houston'
    AND l.zone_type = 'hub'
    AND l.timestamp >= '2025-01-01' AND l.timestamp < '2026-01-01'
)
SELECT
  COUNT(*) AS hours,
  SUM(load_mw) AS mwh,
  SUM(load_mw*lmp) AS energy_cost,
  AVG(lmp) AS simple_avg_lmp,
  SUM(load_mw*lmp)/SUM(load_mw) AS load_weighted_lmp,
  AVG(load_mw) AS avg_mw,
  MAX(load_mw) AS max_mw
FROM hourly;

tool_input.query:
WITH sys AS (
  SELECT timestamp, SUM(load_mw) AS ercot_load_mw
  FROM ercot_load
  WHERE is_day_ahead=false
    AND timestamp >= '2025-06-01' AND timestamp < '2025-10-01'
  GROUP BY timestamp
), ranked AS (
  SELECT *, EXTRACT(MONTH FROM timestamp)::int AS month,
         ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY EXTRACT(MONTH FROM timestamp)::int ORDER BY ercot_load_mw DESC, timestamp) AS rn
  FROM sys
)
SELECT month, timestamp, ercot_load_mw, EXTRACT(HOUR FROM timestamp)::int AS hour
FROM ranked
WHERE rn=1
ORDER BY month;

WITH p AS (
  SELECT * FROM (VALUES
    (0, 0.62,0.58,0.55,0.58),(1,0.60,0.56,0.53,0.56),(2,0.58,0.54,0.51,0.54),(3,0.57,0.53,0.50,0.53),
    (4,0.58,0.54,0.51,0.54),(5,0.62,0.58,0.55,0.58),(6,0.68,0.64,0.61,0.64),(7,0.74,0.71,0.69,0.71),
    (8,0.81,0.79,0.78,0.79),(9,0.88,0.87,0.88,0.87),(10,0.94,0.94,0.96,0.94),(11,0.98,0.99,1.00,0.99),
    (12,1.00,1.00,1.00,1.00),(13,1.00,1.00,1.00,1.00),(14,0.99,1.00,1.00,1.00),(15,0.97,0.99,1.00,0.99),
    (16,0.95,0.97,0.99,0.97),(17,0.93,0.95,0.97,0.95),(18,0.90,0.92,0.95,0.92),(19,0.86,0.88,0.91,0.88),
    (20,0.81,0.83,0.86,0.83),(21,0.75,0.77,0.80,0.77),(22,0.70,0.71,0.73,0.71),(23,0.66,0.64,0.61,0.64)
  ) AS v(hr,winter,spring,summer,fall)
), hourly AS (
  SELECT l.timestamp, l.lmp,
         200.0 * CASE
           WHEN EXTRACT(MONTH FROM l.timestamp) IN (12,1,2) THEN p.winter
           WHEN EXTRACT(MONTH FROM l.timestamp) IN (3,4,5) THEN p.spring
           WHEN EXTRACT(MONTH FROM l.timestamp) IN (6,7,8) THEN p.summer
           ELSE p.fall END AS load_mw,
         CASE
           WHEN EXTRACT(MONTH FROM l.timestamp) IN (12,1,2) THEN 'Winter'
           WHEN EXTRACT(MONTH FROM l.timestamp) IN (3,4,5) THEN 'Spring'
           WHEN EXTRACT(MONTH FROM l.timestamp) IN (6,7,8) THEN 'Summer'
           ELSE 'Fall' END AS season
  FROM ercot_lmp l
  JOIN p ON p.hr = EXTRACT(HOUR FROM l.timestamp)::int
  WHERE l.is_day_ahead = true AND l.zone_name = 'houston' AND l.zone_type = 'hub'
    AND l.timestamp >= '2025-01-01' AND l.timestamp < '2026-01-01'
)
SELECT season, COUNT(*) hours, SUM(load_mw) mwh, AVG(lmp) avg_lmp, SUM(load_mw*lmp)/SUM(load_mw) lw_lmp, SUM(load_mw*lmp) energy_cost
FROM hourly GROUP BY season ORDER BY MIN(timestamp);

  FROM ercot_load

  FROM sys
)
SELECT month, timestamp, ercot_load_mw, EXTRACT(HOUR FROM timestamp)::int AS hour
FROM ranked
WHERE rn=1
ORDER BY month;

steps.content:
## Result

For a 200 MW transmission-connected Houston data center exposed to ERCOT Houston Hub day-ahead prices, I estimate **2025 annual electricity cost for energy + transmission-related charges only** at:

> **$61.55 million in 2025**  
> **= $48.60 million energy + $12.95 million transmission**

This equals an all-in energy-plus-transmission cost of about **$44.45/MWh** over the modeled 2025 load.

---

## Key assumptions

1. **Load shape:** I scaled the user-provided seasonal hourly profile by **200 MW**. Source: user-provided profiles.
2. **Season mapping:** Winter = Dec–Feb, Spring = Mar–May, Summer = Jun–Aug, Fall = Sep–Nov. Assumption.
3. **Energy price:** ERCOT **Houston Hub day-ahead hourly LMPs** for 2025. Source: `ercot_db.ercot_lmp`, filtered to `zone_name='houston'`, `zone_type='hub'`, `is_day_ahead=true`, 2025 calendar year.
4. **4CP billing rule:** 2025 transmission billing demand is based on the **previous year’s average 4CP** demand, i.e., the load coincident with ERCOT system peaks in June, July, August, and September of 2024. Source: 16 Tex. Admin. Code §25.192(d), which states the current year’s average 4CP demand is used to bill transmission service for the next year and defines the ERCOT average 4CP as the average of coincident peaks in June–September. URL: https://www.law.cornell.edu/regulations/texas/16-Tex-Admin-Code-SS-25-192
5. **2025 ERCOT transmission rate:** I used **$66.76/kW-year** as the ERCOT-wide 2025 transmission rate. Source: NRG “Transmission in ERCOT: Are Residential Customers Paying Too Much?” Feb. 25, 2025, stating “ERCOT’s transmission rate is currently $66.76 per kilowatt-year,” citing Commission Staff’s petition to set wholesale transmission service charges. URL: https://www.nrg.com/assets/documents/energy-policy/ercot-transmission-costs-and-rate-design-remarks-on-feb-25-2025.pdf. The relevant PUCT docket is **Docket No. 57491**, “Commission Staff’s Petition to Set 2025 Wholesale Transmission Service Charges for ERCOT.” Source: Texas PUC Interchange search result for Control No. 57491: https://interchange.puc.texas.gov/search/filings/?ControlNumber=57491
6. Excludes all other charges: distribution, losses, ancillary services, ERCOT admin/uplift, taxes, REP margin, power factor/reactive charges, backup service, and interconnection-specific charges. Assumption based on the user’s “energy and transmission-related charges only” instruction.

---

## Energy cost calculation

Using the modeled hourly load and 2025 ERCOT Houston Hub day-ahead LMPs:

| Metric | Value | Source |
|---|---:|---|
| Annual modeled load | **1,384,578 MWh** | User load profile scaled to 200 MW; hourly calculation |
| 2025 Houston Hub DA load-weighted price | **$35.10/MWh** | `ercot_db.ercot_lmp` |
| Energy cost | **$48,596,802** | `ercot_db.ercot_lmp` × modeled load |

Seasonal energy-cost detail:

| Season | MWh | Load-weighted Houston Hub DA price | Energy cost |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| Winter | 344,160 | $30.07/MWh | $10,347,355 |
| Spring | 348,204 | $37.65/MWh | $13,109,809 |
| Summer | 347,576 | $38.49/MWh | $13,379,833 |
| Fall | 344,638 | $34.12/MWh | $11,759,805 |
| **Total** | **1,384,578** | **$35.10/MWh** | **$48,596,802** |

Source for hourly prices: `ercot_db.ercot_lmp`, 2025, Houston Hub, day-ahead.

---

## Transmission 4CP calculation

Under ERCOT 4CP rules, transmission billing is based on the customer’s average coincident demand during ERCOT’s June, July, August, and September system peaks. Source: 16 Tex. Admin. Code §25.192(d): https://www.law.cornell.edu/regulations/texas/16-Tex-Admin-Code-SS-25-192

For 2025 transmission billing, I used the 2024 ERCOT system 4CP hours from `ercot_db.ercot_load`:

| Month | ERCOT system peak hour used | ERCOT load | Data center profile hour | Data center demand |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| June 2024 | 2024-06-30 17:00 | 79,697.9 MW | Summer hour 17 = 0.97 | 194 MW |
| July 2024 | 2024-07-01 17:00 | 81,010.1 MW | Summer hour 17 = 0.97 | 194 MW |
| August 2024 | 2024-08-20 17:00 | 85,198.9 MW | Summer hour 17 = 0.97 | 194 MW |
| September 2024 | 2024-09-19 16:00 | 77,779.5 MW | Fall hour 16 = 0.97 | 194 MW |

Average 4CP demand:

\[
\frac{194 + 194 + 194 + 194}{4} = 194 \text{ MW}
\]

Transmission charge:

\[
194{,}000 \text{ kW} \times 66.76 \frac{\$}{\text{kW-year}}
= \$12{,}951{,}440
\]

Sources: `ercot_db.ercot_load` for ERCOT system peak hours; user-provided load profiles; $66.76/kW-year from NRG Feb. 25, 2025 remarks citing PUCT 2025 wholesale transmission charge petition.

---

## Total 2025 cost

| Component | Cost |
|---|---:|
| Energy at ERCOT Houston Hub day-ahead prices | **$48,596,802** |
| ERCOT 4CP transmission charge | **$12,951,440** |
| **Total annual electricity cost, energy + transmission only** | **$61,548,242** |

\[
\text{Effective cost} =
\frac{61{,}548{,}242}{1{,}384{,}578 \text{ MWh}}
= \mathbf{44.45 \$/MWh}
\]

**Final answer: approximately $61.55 million for 2025, or $44.45/MWh, considering energy and ERCOT 4CP transmission-related charges only.**
